CREATE TABLE xper_entities (id INT PRIMARY KEY, xml_data LONG VARCHAR);
SELECT xper_cut((SELECT xml_data FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 1)) AS cut_data_1;
SELECT xper_cut((SELECT xml_data FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 2)) AS cut_data_2;
SELECT xper_cut((SELECT xml_data FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 3)) AS cut_data_3;
SELECT xper_cut(xml_data) FROM xper_entities WHERE id IN (1, 3);
SELECT DISTINCT xper_cut(x.xml_data) FROM xper_entities x JOIN (SELECT * FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 2) y ON x.id <> y.id;
SELECT xper_cut((SELECT xml_data FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 1)) FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 2;
SELECT xper_cut((SELECT xml_data FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 1 AND xml_data IS NOT NULL)) AS valid_cut;
SELECT xper_cut((SELECT xml_data FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 1)) AS first_cut, xper_cut((SELECT xml_data FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 2)) AS second_cut;
SELECT xper_cut((SELECT xml_data FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 2)) AS second_cut FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE xml_backups (backup_id INT PRIMARY KEY, backup_data LONG VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO xml_backups (backup_id, backup_data) SELECT 1, xper_cut(xml_data) FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 1;
INSERT INTO xml_backups (backup_id, backup_data) SELECT 2, xper_cut(xml_data) FROM xper_entities WHERE id = 2;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM xml_backups WHERE backup_data IS NOT NULL;
